architecture_notes: CopyQueue is a facade: configure threads settings to all children; enqueue feeds the scheduler, which drives the swap transfer; wait blocks on the scheduler; recover_swap delegates to the swap transfer; cleanup delegates to the cleanup worker.
Transport supplies every per-peer filesystem primitive, so each child orchestrates Transport calls and never branches on peer scheme.

children.name: CopyScheduler
children.responsibility: Schedule queued copies concurrently under one run-global copy-slot limit and retry each failed copy up to its per-copy try limit, accepting new copies while earlier ones run and never blocking non-copy work on a full slot limit.

children.name: SwapTransfer
children.responsibility: Perform one file copy by streaming through SWAP staging so the destination is never written in place, run the ordered SWAP replacement and its error obligations, and recover any existing SWAP directory through the five-state recovery machine.

children.name: StagingCleanup
children.responsibility: Purge aged BAK and TMP entries under a peer's .kitchensync/ by the timestamp in each directory name against the BAK and TMP retention limits, leaving recent entries and never aging out SWAP.
